key_insight: Making BaTiO3 membranes thinner suppresses visible cracking, but it shifts failure mode toward dense nanoscale wrinkling, revealing a clear thickness-dependent trade-off between fracture resistance and morphological stability.
engineering_relevance: The results provide practical thickness-selection guidelines for integrating freestanding ferroelectric oxide membranes into silicon-compatible low-power electronics and memory devices.
method: Varied BaTiO3 membrane thickness from 15 nm to 5 nm after release.
Adjusted polymer support thickness for 10 nm membranes to study its effect on wrinkling.
Used piezoresponse force microscopy to track post-release ferroelectric contrast evolution.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that 5 nm membranes largely suppress optically visible cracking, while 10 nm membranes show wrinkle density changes with polymer thickness and time-dependent PFM contrast evolution after release.
watch_item: Reducing cracking does not guarantee better device quality because ultrathin membranes can develop dense wrinkling and time-dependent apparent ferroelectric response from morphology, charge screening, and strain relaxation.
paper_title: Thickness-dependent crack suppression and wrinkle formation in freestanding BaTiO3 membranes
